questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (c), all follow the same logic as follows: 2*6 + 5*3 = 12+15=27 7*9 + 8*1 = 63+8=71 5*0 + 2*1 = 0+2=2

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except humus all others are the forms of rocks. Humus is the organic component of soil.

questions.question: Six boxes of different colour red, purple, yellow, blue, black and green were placed in a row. Each box weighed differently. Black box weighed more than yellow box but less than green box. There were only 2 boxes between blue box and yellow box where blue box weighs more than yellow box. Red box weighed more than the blue box but less than the purple box. Which box was the 3ʳᵈ lightest?
questions.explanation: There were only 2 boxes between blue box and yellow box where blue box weighs more than yellow box. Blue > __ > __ > Yellow Red box weighed more than the blue box but less than the purple box. Purple> Red> Blue > __ > __ > Yellow Black box weighed more than yellow box but less than green box Purple> Red> Blue > Green > Black > Yellow Thus, the green box was the 3ʳᵈ lightest

questions.question: In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two signs. 440 X 10 - 330 + 100 ÷ 5 = 214
questions.explanation: On interchanging X and ÷ signs we get, LHS = 440 ÷ 10 - 330 + 100 X 5 = 44 - 330 + 500 = 214

questions.question: If 150 + 16 % 100 = -110 and 240 + 10 % 300 = -160, then in the expression 50 + 12 % 18 = M, what is the value of 'M'?
questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: The middle number is multiplied by 10 before the mathematical operation. i.e., 50 - 12*10 - 18 = M M = -88

questions.question: In the following diagram, the square represents girls, the circle represents tall persons, the triangle is for tennis players and the rectangle stands for the swimmers. Which letter represents tall girls who are swimmers but don't play tennis? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/8386/image011.png)
questions.explanation: Tall girl, who are swimmers are represented by the region common to the square, circle and the rectangle i.e., G and H. But, according to the given conditions, the girls shouldn't be tennis players. Thus, the region satisfying the given conditions is G.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices is represented first by its row and next by its column. e.g. 'U' can be represented by 01, 14 etc. and 'M' can be represented by 57, 58 etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word MARBLE. MATRIX I 01234 0EUBCR 1RBCEU 2CERBU 3BUERC 4RECUB MATRIX II 56789 5OLMHA 6AHLMO 7MOALH 8HLMOA 9LAOMH
questions.explanation: From the matrix, we have M → 57, 68, 75, 87 and 98 A → 59, 65, 77, 89 and 96 R → 04, 10, 22, 33 and 40 B → 02, 11, 23, 30 and 44 L → 56, 67, 78, 86 and 95 E → 00, 13, 21, 32 and 41 Only the option 3 has the codes of the letters in the same sequence.

questions.question: Some fans are crazy. All fans are loyal. Some hooligans are fans. Conclusion I : All hooligans are crazy. Conclusion II: Some crazy are fans. Conclusion III: All loyal are crazy. Conclusion IV : Some hooligans are crazy.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/94250/image011.jpg) From the above venn diagram we can say that only conclusion II is true.

questions.question: Bacteriophobia : Bacteria : : ? : Darkness
questions.explanation: Fear of bacteria is known as Bacteriophobia Fear of darkness is known as Lygophobia.

questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p743/image034.png) Opposite of % is @, opposite of $ is #, opposite of = is +.

questions.question: Select the related word/letters/number from the given alternatives. 9216:225:: 5929:?
From the given alternative words, select the word which can be formed using the letters of the given word 'narcotics'?
questions.explanation: Only option 1 contains all the letters present in NARCOTICS.

questions.question: LEATHER : ODDSKDU : : VEHICLE : ?
questions.explanation: LEATHER VEHICLE +3-1+3-1+3-1+3 +3-1+3-1+3-1+3 ODDSKDU YDKHFKH

questions.question: Kamala is the mother of Ravi and Saloni is the mother of Suraj. Suraj's father, Madan, has only one daughter Kamala. If Suraj is the husband of Pragya, how is Pragya related to Ravi?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/49188/image001.jpg) Pragya is Ravi's aunt.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Universe 2. Undisputed 3. Unfounded 4. Undying 5. Untiring
questions.explanation: This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary. Undisputed> Undying> Unfounded> Universe> Untiring

questions.question: How many squares are there in the given figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image035.png)
questions.explanation: Total number of squares = number of squares of 1X1 + number of squares of 2X2 + number of squares of 3X3 + number of squares of 4X4 + number of squares of 5X5 Total number of squares = (5*5) + (4*4) + (3*3) + (2*2) + 1 = 55

questions.question: Select the missing number(s) from the given responses. 3, 9, 22, 43, 73, ?
Find the odd word/letters/number pair from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: For option 1, DNWE, D+10 =N, N+9 = W, W+8 =E. For option 2, JTCK, J+10 =T, T+9 = C, C+8 =K. For option 3, RBKS, R+10 =B, B+9 = K, K+8 =S. For option 4, VFOV, V+10 =F, F+9 = O, O+8 =W. Hence the option 4 is the odd one.

questions.question: Which one set of letters/numbers when sequentially placed in the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? p q r _ t s _ q _ p q _ q p _
questions.explanation: The series is pqrst srqp pqr qp p.

questions.question: In a certain code, BREAD is written as JFIUD. How is PEARL written in that code?
questions.explanation: First the letters are reversed, then the letters are increased by 6,5,4,3,2 respectively. BREAD → DAERB → JFIUD Similarly, PEARL → LRAEP → RWEHR
